data.title: GSK subsidiary Tesaro, AnaptysBio provide update on contract claim litigation
data.description:
Tesaro, a subsidiary of GSK (GSK), and AnaptysBio (ANAB) provided an update on AnaptysBio litigation involving an anticipatory breach of contract claim.

GSK plc (GSK) subsidiary Tesaro said the Delaware Court of Chancery has granted a motion to dismiss AnaptysBio’s (ANAB) claim alleging anticipatory breach of contract.

The court’s ruling does not address the merits of the principal contractual dispute between the parties and has no impact on Tesaro's remaining claim against AnaptysBio for declaratory judgment, a statement on Monday said.

AnaptysBio (ANAB) in a statement on April 24, stated that the Delaware Chancery Court has dismissed Tesaro’s anticipatory breach of contract claim against Anaptys. "The ruling agrees with Anaptys’ position that it has never repudiated the Collaboration and Exclusive License Agreement (“Collaboration Agreement”) with Tesaro, a subsidiary of GSK, governing the development and commercialization of Jemperli," the company said.

The trial to adjudicate Anaptys’ contract claims and right to seek reversion of Jemperli against Tesaro/GSK is scheduled for July 14-17, 2026, AnaptysBio (ANAB) noted.

Tesaro initiated the litigation following allegations made by AnaptysBio (ANAB) that Tesaro has not fulfilled certain requirements of a 2014 license agreement, and that AnaptysBio intended to revoke Tesaro's licence for dostarlimab.

Jemperli, which is the brand name for dostarlimab, is currently approved in over 35 countries for use in certain endometrial cancers, the most common gynecologic cancer in the U.S.

Updates story to add AnaptysBio's statement.

data.title: Enphase Q1 earnings on deck: What to expect
data.description:
Enphase Energy (ENPH) will report its results for the first quarter on Tuesday, after markets close.

Wall Street expects the company to post EPS of 45 cents, on revenue of $282.4M, representing a year-over-year decline of about 21%.

Enphase Energy’s quarter was characterized by improving analyst sentiment and a more constructive outlook on demand and margins, with several upgrades following stronger visibility into the business. The stock also gained on upbeat guidance and positive revenue expectations, as firms including Jefferies and others raised ratings on the back of improving fundamentals.

The company continued to emphasize longer-term growth opportunities in energy storage, including a targeted $2 billion battery retrofit market in the Netherlands and the rollout of next-generation batteries alongside AI-driven customer tools. At the same time, it undertook cost-cutting measures, including layoffs of around 160 employees as part of a broader restructuring effort.

Overall, the period reflected a mix of improving forward-looking confidence, strategic expansion in storage and software, and ongoing efforts to streamline operations.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system, ENPH is rated Hold with an overall score of 3.12 out of 5, reflecting a B grade in terms of revisions, but it has an F in terms of growth.

A recent Seeking Alpha analysis said Enphase’s near-term performance remains challenged by weak demand in key regions and regulatory uncertainty, even as operational improvements and emerging growth drivers provide some support, pointing out that “even though the company is seeing revenue drops, it stays relatively stable on gross margins, given all the headwinds it faces.”

Over the past two years, ENPH has beaten EPS estimates 50% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 50% of the time.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen 14 upward revisions and three downward revisions, while revenue estimates have seen 14 upward revisions and three downward moves.

data.title: Americold Realty Trust, Lineage downgraded at Barclays on industry challenges
data.description:
Americold Realty Trust (COLD) and Lineage (LINE) were downgraded at Barclays over 'persistent challenges' that the cold storage industry faces.

LINE shares were trading 2.10% lower at $36.36 after the market opened on Monday, while COLD fell 1.87% to $12.59.

The investment bank lowered its rating on the stocks to Underweight from Overweight and Equal Weight on Americold and Lineage, respectively. Price targets were set at $12.83 and $37.14.

"Rising fertilizer costs add to the persistent challenges the cold storage industry faces: excess supply, GLP-1 adoption, food inflation, population deceleration, tariffs, diminished cattle herds, etc.," said analysts Brendan Lynch, Eileen Gao, and Annabelle Ayer.

"Our checks suggest leasing and transaction volume slowed in February and March due to the uncertainty," said the analysts.

"We see greater risk to 2027 consensus estimates, due to the protracted nature of the industry challenges. While we do not anticipate a guidance cut, we do think weak 1Q26 numbers could weigh on sentiment/trading multiples," said the research note.

Barclays noted that the downgrade did not result from a negative call on the management team.

Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system grade COLD as Hold, while SA authors see the stock as Buy. Meanwhile, sell-side analysts rate LINE as Hold, SA authors as Buy, and Quant as Sell.

data.title: Goldman Sachs trims 2026 IPO outlook amid volatility, pipeline risks
data.description:
Goldman Sachs expects about 100 IPOs raising $160B in 2026, down from a prior forecast of 120 deals for the same total value, as market volatility and geopolitical risks weigh on issuance.

From a macro perspective, the bank’s IPO Barometer stands at 136, signaling that conditions remain supportive for new listings. However, recent equity market volatility and geopolitical uncertainty are likely to result in fewer deals than previously anticipated, tempering expectations for a full recovery in issuance.

So far in 2026, IPO activity has shown signs of improvement, with 25 U.S. offerings raising about $14B, an increase of nearly 80% in both deal count and proceeds compared with the same period last year. Despite this pickup, Goldman said execution conditions remain uneven across sectors.

Structural challenges within the pipeline are also emerging. The bank noted that roughly 20% of the IPO backlog since 2025 is concentrated in software companies, which could act as a headwind if investor appetite for the sector remains selective.

From a micro perspective, several large private companies are expected to go public in the coming quarters, potentially supporting overall volumes. However, uncertainty around the timing and size of these deals introduces two-sided risks to the 2026 outlook.

Overall, Goldman said the pace of IPO activity will depend on market stability, investor demand, and how quickly large deals come to market.

More on IPOs

  • From OpenAI To SpaceX: The IPO ETF Bet On A 2026 Go-Public Surge
  • SA Asks: Which AI-related companies will hold IPOs this year?
  • Track this: Strava is laying the groundwork for an IPO
  • Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating on Discord
  • Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating on Renaissance IPO ETF

data.title: Western Digital, Seagate maintain clear path to higher earnings; BofA ups price objectives
data.description:
Western Digital (WDC) and Seagate Technology (STX) both maintain clear trajectories to higher earnings, prompting Bank of America to increase its price objectives on both stocks.

"Hard disk drive supply remains tight as manufacturers are not adding unit capacity, and we see this as a structural change," said BofA analysts, led by Wamsi Mohan, in a Monday investor note. "As such, we continue to see demand exceeding supply and see continued scope for OEMs to raise prices."

BofA increased its price objective on Seagate to $700 from $605 and Western Digital to $495 from $415. In a bull case scenario, BofA finds that both companies could see significantly higher earnings per share totals by 2028.

"We estimate that the current stock price of around $587 factors in a C28 EPS of $29 (derived using nearline $/TB CAGR of 4% and C28 OM of 47%) and assuming a 20x multiple (which we see as appropriate for the growth and margin profile of STX)," the analysts said of Seagate. "But, in a bull case, we see the potential for EPS of $45 ($/TB reaching $20 in C28 with a 3-year $/TB CAGR of 16% and OM of 55%)."

For Western Digital, BofA said, "We estimate that the current stock price of around $400 factors in a C28 EPS of about $20 (derived using nearline $/TB CAGR of 0% (no growth) and C28 OM of 45%) and assuming a 20x multiple (which we see as appropriate for the growth and margin profile of WDC). But, in a bull case, we see the potential for EPS of $33 ($/TB reaching $20 in C28 with a 3-year CAGR of 12% and OM of 55%)."

Visibility is also improving for both companies as they lock in long-term contracts with customers.

"The market remains an oligopoly (with three OEMs providing hard disk drives), and we see a low threat of new entrants coming into the market," Wamsi added.

The share prices of Seagate and Western Digital have both more than doubled year-to-date.

data.title: How to play the K-shaped economy in the restaurant sector
data.description:
JPMorgan believes the K-shaped economy remains firmly in place, especially in the restaurant sector.

Analyst John Ivankoe noted that the top 40% of consumers, who generate approximately 65% of all restaurant sales, are seeing increased spending power, supported by rising home values, robust stock market participation, and outsized benefits from recent tax policy changes and refunds. Meanwhile, Ivankoe highlighted that the bottom 60% of consumers are experiencing stagnant wage growth, with many unable to participate in recent wealth creation. "About 35% of households have not benefited from asset price appreciation. Legislative changes — including the expiration of ACA tax credits and expanded SNAP work requirements — are expected to further pressure this cohort," he warned.

Adding it all up, Ivankoe and his team said the combined effect of the policies is net negative for the bottom 70% of income earners, while only the top 30% can offset headwinds through tax cuts and market asset gains.

The recommendation to investors is to add to positions in its favorite names, such as Domino's Pizza (DPZ), Dutch Bros (BROS), and Brinker International (EAT). While the level of Treasury yields impacts restaurant sector valuations, the firm still sees value in shares of Darden Restaurants (DRI), McDonald's (MCD), Restaurant Brands International (QSR), and Yum! Brands (YUM). While noting valuation is stretched, JPMorgan is sticking with bullish views on CAVA Group (CAVA) and Starbucks (SBUX). The firm is less positive on Bloomin' Brands (BLMN), Cheesecake Factory (CAKE), Krispy Kreme (DNUT), and Wendy's (WEN).

data.title: Goldman Sachs cuts IPO forecast; biggest IPOs since 2025 ranked by market cap
data.description:
Goldman Sachs cut its 2026 IPO forecast, now expecting about 100 deals raising $160 billion, down from a prior estimate of 120 offerings, citing geopolitical uncertainty and recent equity market volatility. The bank said the macro backdrop remains supportive but flagged increased selectivity and downside risks to issuance.

data.title: Critical Metals to buy European Lithium in $835M deal, taking full Tanbreez ownership
data.description:
Critical Metals (CRML) up 7.2% in early trading Monday after saying it agreed to acquire all shares of Australian-listed miner ​European Lithium (EULIF) for ~$835M, giving ⁠the U.S. company full ​ownership of Greenland's Tanbreez ​rare earth project.

Critical Metals (CRML) already owned a ​92.5% interest in the ​asset, with European Lithium (EULIF) holding the remaining 7.5% in the project.

Under the deal terms, ​European ​Lithium (EULIF) ⁠shareholders would receive 0.035 shares of ​Critical Metals (CRML) for each ​share held.

Critical Metals (CRML) says the Tanbreez deposit, which contains rare earths such as terbium and dysprosium used in consumer electronics and defense technology, is one of the world's largest known rare earth assets.

The company noted that European Lithium (EULIF) has a ~A$306M (US$219M) cash balance as of March 31; Critical Metals (CRML), whose standalone cash balance totals US$124M, said it will have a robust balance sheet to accelerate the development of Tanbreez.
